description: Verify provide `omnican_node_init()` to bind a zephyr can device, set bitrate, and enable fd mode
Verify return `omnican_err_nodev` from `omnican_node_init()` when the can device is not ready
Verify define `omnican_proto_t` bitmask with one bit per protocol (canopen, j1939, uds, obd2)
Verify define `omnican_err_t` with codes ok, inval, nodev, nomem, timeout, busy, proto, unsupported, io
Verify register as a zephyr west module via `zephyr/module.yml` so `west build` adds include/ to the build path
Verify be buildable against zephyr v3.7.0 with no patches to zephyr itself
Verify register exactly one can receive filter per enabled protocol
Verify dispatch received frames to the correct protocol handler without blocking the can isr
Verify use a zephyr system workqueue for all protocol callback dispatch
Verify auto-enable via config_omnican_frame_router when any protocol is active
Verify support simultaneous reception of 11-bit standard and 29-bit extended can frames on the same router instance
Verify support 29-bit extended can id filtering for canopen fd extended cob-ids and vendor-specific profiles
Verify provide configurable per-protocol can filter masks via kconfig so applications can narrow or broaden reception ranges
Verify implement the nmt state machine with states initialising, pre-operational, operational, and stopped per cia 301
Verify transmit a boot-up message (nmt state 0x00) on transition from initialising to pre-operational
Verify respond to nmt master commands (start, stop, pre-operational, reset node, reset communication) on can id 0x000
Verify implement an sdo server supporting expedited, segmented, and block transfer modes per cia 301
Verify return sdo abort code 0x05040000 on sdo timeout
Verify support tpdo and rpdo with event-driven and synchronisation-triggered transmission modes
Verify support compile-time pdo mappings via the object dictionary
Verify produce emergency messages (can id 0x80 + node_id) with 16-bit error code and 8-byte error register per cia 301
Verify produce heartbeat messages at a kconfig-configurable interval (default 1000 ms) per cia 301
Verify support heartbeat consumer monitoring of remote nodes with error event on timeout
Verify represent the object dictionary as a static c array generated from an od descriptor with no heap allocation
Verify write recommended delay to next `omnican_canopen_process()` call in microseconds via `*next_us`
Verify run the canopen process loop in a dedicated zephyr thread with configurable stack size and priority
Verify provide `omnican_canopen_nmt_change_cb_t` callback invoked on every nmt state transition (both self-initiated and master-commanded)
Verify provide `omnican_canopen_sdo_post_write_cb_t` callback after every successful sdo write so the application can react to od changes
Verify provide `omnican_canopen_rpdo_rx_cb_t` callback when rpdo data is received and mapped into the od
Verify provide `omnican_canopen_emcy_rx_cb_t` callback when an emergency message from a remote node is received
Verify provide `omnican_canopen_sync_cb_t` callback on sync message reception for application-level synchronisation
Verify provide `omnican_canopen_hb_event_cb_t` callback for heartbeat consumer events distinguishing timeout and recovery
Verify provide `omnican_canopen_bootup_rx_cb_t` callback when a remote node boot-up message is received
Verify use 29-bit extended can identifiers and reject 11-bit standard id frames
Verify implement the address-claiming procedure per j1939/81 with 250 ms contention window and 64-bit name priority
Verify prevent pgn transmission until address claiming succeeds
Verify return omnican_err_busy and set addr to null when address claiming fails
Verify support a static pgn routing table populated via `omnican_j1939_register_pgn()` with callback and user-data
Verify invoke pgn callbacks from the system workqueue with source address, data pointer, and length
Verify implement j1939 transport protocol (tp) for 9-1785 byte messages using bam and cmdt modes
Verify implement extended transport protocol (etp) for messages larger than 1785 bytes
Verify maintain tp/etp session state in static context with configurable maximum simultaneous sessions
Verify provide `omnican_j1939_address_result_cb_t` callback reporting address claim success, failure, or address-lost events
Verify provide `omnican_j1939_tp_progress_cb_t` callback reporting bytes transferred during active tp/etp sessions
Verify provide `omnican_j1939_tp_complete_cb_t` callback on tp/etp session completion or abort with status
Verify support j1939 diagnostic pgn reception: dm1 (pgn 0xfeca, active dtcs) and dm11 (pgn 0xfed3, clear active dtcs) via the pgn routing table
Verify support configurable maximum tp/etp single-transfer payload via config_omnican_j1939_tp_max_payload (default 1785 bytes for tp, unlimited for etp)
Verify use zephyr iso-tp for all uds message transport with isotp patch when config_omnican_isotp_patch=y
Verify implement diagnosticsessioncontrol (sid 0x10) and enforce service availability per active session
Verify maintain an s3 timer (default 5000 ms) and revert to default session on expiry
Verify reset the s3 timer on testerpresent (sid 0x3e)
Verify support sid 0x10 diagnosticsessioncontrol
Verify support sid 0x11 ecureset with hardreset, softreset, and keyoffonreset sub-functions
Verify support sid 0x22 readdatabyidentifier with multi-did requests (multiple dids in one 0x22 request)
Verify support sid 0x27 securityaccess with application-supplied seed/key callback
Verify support sid 0x28 communicationcontrol with all sub-functions: enablerxandtx (0x00), enablerxanddisabletx (0x01), disablerxandenabletx (0x02), disablerxandtx (0x03)
Verify support sid 0x2e writedatabyidentifier with application-supplied write validation callback before applying data
Verify support sid 0x31 routinecontrol with application-supplied start, stop, and requestresults executor callbacks per routine id
Verify support sid 0x34 requestdownload
Verify support sid 0x36 transferdata
Verify support sid 0x37 requesttransferexit
Verify support sid 0x85 controldtcsetting with application callback to enable or disable dtc storage
Verify return nrc 0x11 (servicenotsupported) for unregistered sids
Verify never use a hardcoded securityaccess seed/key algorithm; always use an application callback
Verify lock the session and return nrc 0x36 after exceeding config_omnican_uds_max_auth_attempts failed securityaccess attempts
Verify support suppresspositiveresponse sub-function bit (0x80) per iso 14229-1 §7.5.2.2
Verify implement nrc 0x78 (responsepending) when a service handler cannot respond within p2server_max and requires p2starserver_max time
Verify support manufacturer-specific did range (0xf200–0xfeff) via application-registered did handler callbacks with read and write hooks
Verify implement mandatory ecu identification dids: 0xf186 (activesession), 0xf18b (ecumanufacturingdate), 0xf18c (ecuserialnumber), 0xf190 (vin), 0xf195 (systemsupplierecusoftwareversion)
Verify support functional addressing via can id 0x7df for uds broadcast service requests (testerpresent, diagnosticsessioncontrol)
Verify provide `omnican_uds_session_change_cb_t` callback on every session transition with old and new session type
Verify provide `omnican_uds_security_change_cb_t` callback on security access level grant or revocation
Verify provide `omnican_uds_pre_reset_cb_t` callback before ecureset execution allowing application to flush state
Verify support manufacturer-specific nrc values 0x80–0xfe via application nrc provider callback registered per sid
Verify provide `omnican_uds_transfer_progress_cb_t` callback during 0x36 transferdata reporting block sequence and bytes transferred
Verify transmit pid requests on can id 0x7df and collect responses from 0x7e8-0x7ef within configurable timeout
Verify support obd-ii service modes 0x01 through 0x09
Verify provide callback-based non-blocking pid request/response model
Verify support mode 0x01 pids: 0x00 (supported), 0x01 (monitor status), 0x04 (engine load), 0x05 (coolant temp), 0x0c (rpm), 0x0d (vehicle speed), 0x11 (throttle position)
Verify support mode 0x01 pid support bitmap queries (pids 0x00, 0x20, 0x40, 0x60, 0x80, 0xa0, 0xc0) to enumerate ecu-supported pids

Verify support mode 0x01 extended pids: 0x14–0x1b (o2 sensor voltage/trim), 0x1c (obd standard), 0x21 (distance since mil on), 0x2f (fuel level), 0x31 (distance since codes cleared), 0x33 (barometric pressure), 0x46 (ambient air temperature), 0x49 (accelerator position), 0x51 (fuel type), 0x5c (engine oil temperature)
Verify support mode 0x02 (freeze frame) pid requests using the same pid set as mode 0x01 with frame number sub-byte
Verify retrieve mode 0x03 stored dtcs and mode 0x07 pending dtcs as decoded fault codes per sae j2012 (p/c/b/u + 4-digit hex)
Verify support mode 0x04 clear all emission-related dtcs with application completion callback
Verify support mode 0x06 on-board monitoring test results (obdmid and obdmid test results) via callback
Verify support mode 0x09 vehicle information: infotype 0x02 (vin), 0x04 (calibration id), 0x06 (cvn), 0x0a (ecu name)
Verify aggregate responses from multiple ecus for broadcast requests and deliver each ecu response individually via per-ecu callback
Verify support ecu-specific physical addressing: send to 0x7e0–0x7e7 and receive from the corresponding 0x7e8–0x7ef response can id
Verify provide `omnican_obd2_dtc_cb_t` callback delivering decoded dtc records (fault code, status byte) from mode 0x03/0x07 responses
Verify provide `omnican_obd2_timeout_cb_t` callback when a pid request receives no response within the configured timeout
Verify extend canopen module with cia 1301 fd features; require config_omnican_canopen=y and config_can_fd_mode=y
Verify support can fd data payloads of up to 64 bytes for higher-throughput pdo transfers
Verify maintain backward compatibility with classical 8-byte canopen nodes when fd is not negotiated
Verify implement a workaround for zephyr issue #86025 using separate tx/rx socket contexts with a forwarding shim
Verify default config_omnican_isotp_patch to y when config_omnican_uds or config_omnican_obd2 is enabled
Verify never use dynamic heap allocation (k_malloc, malloc); all state statically allocated or via net_buf pools
Verify handle can frame data via zephyr net_buf from a pool sized by config_omnican_net_buf_count (default 16)
Verify all protocol context structures shall be opaque to the caller but caller-allocated
Verify all protocol handlers execute in a zephyr workqueue context, not the can isr
